title: Add TLS/mTLS for Keystone to MariaDB Galera and MaxScale
description:
# GitHub Issue #320: feat: TLS/mTLS for Keystone ↔ MariaDB (Galera + MaxScale)

**Description:**

Today the Keystone API → MariaDB path is plaintext TCP on 3306 end-to-end. This issue adds opt-in TLS (and mTLS) between every Keystone-owned workload that opens a MySQL connection and the `openstack-db` MariaDB Galera cluster (including the MaxScale proxy hop), driven by a new `spec.database.tls` block on the Keystone CRD and a `spec.tls` block on the MariaDB CR. The data plane (pymysql / SQLAlchemy / `keystone-manage`) does not change — only the connection string, the mounted certificate material, and the MariaDB/MaxScale TLS posture.

One correction up front: the issue body cites the pre-CC-0080 design. CC-0080 (commit history; see `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_dbconnection_secret.go` header comment) already refactored the DSN out of the ConfigMap. The accurate, current concrete boundaries are:

1. **DSN builder — already exists.** `reconcile_dbconnection_secret.go:97-104` builds `connURL := &url.URL{Scheme:"mysql+pymysql", User:url.UserPassword(...), Host:resolveDatabaseHost(keystone), Path:...Database, RawQuery:"charset=utf8"}` and writes `connStr` into the derived `corev1.Secret` `<name>-db-connection` under key `"connection"` (`dbConnectionSecretKey`, line 41). **This issue adds:** appending `ssl_ca`/`ssl_cert`/`ssl_key` (and, depending on `mode`, `ssl_verify_cert`/`ssl_verify_identity`) to `RawQuery` when `spec.database.tls.enabled` is true, pointing at the in-pod mount path of the client-cert volume — *not* at operator-read bytes. The reconciler never reads the keypair; it only references file paths.

2. **`[database].connection` placeholder — already exists.** `reconcile_config.go:124` writes `"connection": dbConnectionPlaceholder` (`mysql+pymysql://placeholder`, line 58) into the immutable ConfigMap; the real URL is injected at runtime via `OS_DATABASE__CONNECTION` (`reconcile_deployment.go:35`, `buildDBConnectionEnvVar` lines 42-56). **This issue does not change the placeholder** — the TLS args ride on the derived-Secret value, so the ConfigMap and env-override path are untouched. The issue's reference to `reconcile_config.go:74-81` is stale.

3. **`DatabaseSpec` — already exists, single source.** The canonical struct is `internal/common/types/types.go:21-35` (`DatabaseSpec` with `ClusterRef`, `Host`, `Port`, `Database`, `SecretRef`). `operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_types.go:62` embeds it as `commonv1.DatabaseSpec` (there is no separate keystone-local mirror; the issue has this backwards). **This issue adds:** a `TLS *DatabaseTLSSpec` pointer field on `DatabaseSpec` in `internal/common/types/types.go`, with `Enabled bool`, `CABundleSecretRef`, `ClientCertSecretRef`, and `Mode` (enum `prefer|require|verify-ca|verify-full`). Adding it to the shared struct (not keystone-locally) keeps it consistent with how `ClusterRef`/`Host`/`Port` already live there and reusable by future operators, at the cost of a wider deepcopy/CRD-regen blast radius (see Affected Areas).

4. **`resolveDatabaseHost` / `dbPort` — already exist.** `reconcile_config.go:308-327` resolves managed-mode host as `<clusterRef>.<ns>.svc:<port>` and brownfield as `<host>:<port>`. **This issue adds nothing here**; `verify-full` mode relies on the certificate SAN matching this hostname, which is a MariaDB-CR/cert-manager concern, not a host-resolution change.

5. **cert-manager `Certificate` plumbing — exists but unused in production.** `internal/common/tls/EnsureCertificate` (`internal/common/tls/tls.go:28`) and `IsCertificateReady` are fully implemented and unit/integration-tested (`tls_test.go`, `integration_test.go`) but have **zero production call sites** today (verified: only references are the package itself). **This issue adds** the first production consumer: a new sub-reconciler that issues a cert-manager `Certificate` for the Keystone DB client keypair via `tls.EnsureCertificate`, gated on `spec.database.tls.enabled`.

6. **Pod/Job cert mounts — do not exist.** `buildKeystoneDeployment` (`reconcile_deployment.go:144`) mounts only `config`, `fernet-keys`, `credential-keys` volumes. `buildDBJob` (`reconcile_database.go:789`) — shared by `buildDBSyncJob`, `buildExpandJob`, `buildMigrateJob`, `buildContractJob`, `buildSchemaCheckJob` — mounts only `config`. `buildBootstrapJob` (`reconcile_bootstrap.go`) runs an inline `python3` pre-insert script doing `pymysql.connect(host=url.hostname, ...)` (lines ~95-115) then `keystone-manage bootstrap`. **This issue adds** a read-only client-cert volume (from the cert-manager-issued Secret) mounted into all of these, plus a server-CA mount, at a fixed path (e.g. `/etc/keystone/db-tls/`). The bootstrap pre-insert script must additionally translate the `ssl_*` query params into pymysql's `ssl` connect kwarg, since it parses the URL by hand and `pymysql.connect` takes `ssl=` (a dict), not `ssl_ca=`.

7. **MariaDB CR TLS — does not exist.** `deploy/flux-system/infrastructure/mariadb.yaml` has `spec.replicas/galera/maxScale/storage/metrics` but no `spec.tls`. **This issue adds** `spec.tls` (`enabled`, `required`, `serverCASecretRef`, `clientCASecretRef`, and MaxScale TLS) per the mariadb-operator `k8s.mariadb.com/v1alpha1` API (mariadb-operator `v0.38.1`, `operators/keystone/go.mod:10`). The kind overlay `deploy/kind/infrastructure/kustomization.yaml` already strategic-merge-patches this MariaDB CR (disables Galera/MaxScale, 1 replica); the TLS block must be consistent there so `WITH_*` kind paths and CI exercise TLS, or the divergence must be documented (project pattern: *Document intentional environment divergence in overlays*).

8. **Operator RBAC for cert-manager — does not exist.** `keystone_controller.go:160-179` kubebuilder markers and the centralized Helm RBAC `keystone-operator.rbacRules` (`helm/keystone-operator/templates/_helpers.tpl:66-185`, included by both `clusterrole.yaml` and `role.yaml` per CC-0043) grant `k8s.mariadb.com` databases/users/grants/mariadbs but **no `cert-manager.io` verbs**. **This issue adds** `cert-manager.io` `certificates` (`get;list;watch;create;update;patch;delete`) to *both* the kubebuilder markers and `rbacRules` in lockstep — this is the project's BLOCKING pattern *Sync RBAC markers with Helm ClusterRole templates* (CC-0037/CC-0038); a marker-only change ships a 403 at runtime.

9. **Webhook + CRD validation for the new sub-spec — does not exist.** `keystone_webhook.go:238-244` has a defense-in-depth `Database` mutual-exclusivity block mirroring the `+kubebuilder:validation:XValidation` CEL rule on `KeystoneSpec.Database` (`keystone_types.go:57`). **This issue adds** matching admission validation for `database.tls` (mode enum membership; if `enabled` then `caBundleSecretRef`/`clientCertSecretRef` semantics), enforced in *both* the webhook `validate()` and a CRD CEL rule with identical accept/reject semantics (project patterns *Apply defense-in-depth checks consistently to all similarly-marked fields* and *Ensure CRD schema validation mirrors webhook validation semantics*, CC-0011/CC-0039).

Recommended CA topology (the issue says "pin a cert-manager Issuer" and "re-use `serverCASecretRef`/`clientCASecretRef`"): introduce one cert-manager CA `Issuer`/`ClusterIssuer` for the OpenStack DB trust domain (a CA keypair, *not* the existing `selfsigned-cluster-issuer` which is leaf-self-signed). MariaDB's `spec.tls.serverCASecretRef` and `clientCASecretRef` reference cert-manager-issued CA Secrets from that issuer; the Keystone operator issues its client `Certificate` from the *same* issuer so MaxScale/MariaDB trust it via `clientCASecretRef`. mariadb-operator's own auto-CA generation is the alternative and is called out in Non-Goals. The `mode → connect_args` mapping the issue explicitly defers is specified in Acceptance Criteria.

This is greenfield: no branch, feature file, or `ssl_ca` token exists anywhere in the tree. The sibling "OpenBao mTLS" and "Memcached TLS" issues and "operator's known TLS gaps" referenced in the issue have no concrete CC-XXXX IDs in `.planwerk/` and are preserved here per the issue as cross-references only.

**Motivation:**

Credentials and every token-validation query currently traverse the pod network in cleartext. `mariadb-operator` v0.38.1 already implements `spec.tls` with automatic CA generation, rotation, and `tls.required` enforcement across Galera *and* MaxScale, and the Keystone operator already owns `internal/common/tls.EnsureCertificate` — fully tested but never wired into a production path. The cost of closing this gap is therefore almost entirely integration, not new infrastructure: no data-plane rewrite, no new sidecar, no schema migration.

It matters now because the connection path was *just* refactored (CC-0080) into a single derived-Secret DSN builder — the cleanest possible point to inject `ssl_*` args is the codebase as it stands today; deferring means re-touching `reconcile_dbconnection_secret.go`, every Job builder, and the bootstrap script again later. It is also the reference pattern: this is the first production use of the `tls` package and the first operator-issued client certificate; the OpenBao-mTLS and Memcached-TLS sibling work will copy whatever shape lands here. Skipping it leaves the project's own documented TLS-gap list open and ships a Keystone stack whose primary secret-bearing channel fails the project's *Kubernetes TLS and Certificate Management* and *Secure by Design and Default* review patterns (plaintext-by-default, no in-cluster component TLS).

**Affected Areas:**

- `internal/common/types/types.go` (add `DatabaseTLSSpec` + `TLS *DatabaseTLSSpec` on `DatabaseSpec`; new kubebuilder markers/enum)
- `internal/common/types/zz_generated.deepcopy.go` (regenerated via `make generate-common`)
- `operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_types.go` (CEL `XValidation` on `Database` for the TLS sub-block; doc comment)
- `operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/zz_generated.deepcopy.go` (regenerated via `make generate`)
- `operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_webhook.go` (defense-in-depth `validate()` block + possibly `Default()` for `mode`)
- `operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_webhook_test.go`, `keystone_types_test.go`, `integration_test.go` (validation + accept/reject cases; update `TestValidateCreate_RunsAllValidations` per project pattern)
- `operators/keystone/config/crd/bases/keystone.openstack.c5c3.io_keystones.yaml` **and** `operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/crds/keystone.openstack.c5c3.io_keystones.yaml` (regenerated + synced via `make manifests sync-crds`; project BLOCKING pattern *Regenerate auto-generated files after API type changes*)
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_dbconnection_secret.go` (append `ssl_*` to `connURL.RawQuery` when TLS enabled) + `reconcile_dbconnection_secret_test.go` (assert new DSN; existing exact-string assertions will break — expected blast radius)
- New `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_databasetls.go` + `_test.go` (issue/consume the client `Certificate` via `tls.EnsureCertificate`; new condition type as typed constants per project pattern *Extract repeated condition strings into typed constants*)
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller.go` (insert the new sub-reconciler in dependency order — after `Secrets`, before `DBConnectionSecret`/`Config`/`Deployment`; add it to `subReconcilerConditionTypes` drift maps per CC-0089; add `cert-manager.io` RBAC markers at lines 160-179)
- `reconcile_deployment.go` (`buildKeystoneDeployment`: client-cert + CA volume/mount), `reconcile_database.go` (`buildDBJob`: same mount on all 5 db_sync variants), `reconcile_bootstrap.go` (`buildBootstrapJob`: mount + map `ssl_*` → pymysql `ssl=` dict in the inline script) + their `_test.go` files
- `operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/templates/_helpers.tpl` (`keystone-operator.rbacRules`: add `cert-manager.io/certificates`) + Helm RBAC tests
- `deploy/flux-system/infrastructure/mariadb.yaml` (`spec.tls` + MaxScale TLS), new `deploy/flux-system/infrastructure/db-ca-issuer.yaml` (cert-manager CA Issuer/Certificate) + `deploy/flux-system/infrastructure/kustomization.yaml` (add the new resource)
- `deploy/kind/infrastructure/kustomization.yaml` (keep TLS consistent in the MariaDB strategic-merge patch or document divergence)
- `hack/deploy-infra.sh` (Phase 2 ordering: DB CA Secret must exist before MariaDB starts, mirroring the existing `cluster-issuer.yaml` + `openbao-tls-cert.yaml` Phase-2 apply at lines 997-999)
- New `tests/e2e/keystone/database-tls/` (chainsaw suite + CR fixtures; 1-feature-to-1-E2E-dir convention — every feature under `tests/e2e/keystone/` has one)
- `docs/reference/keystone/keystone-crd.md` (DatabaseSpec table + new `DatabaseTLSSpec` subsection + webhook-validation table + CEL table + E2E inventory), `docs/reference/infrastructure/infrastructure-manifests.md` (MariaDB TLS), new how-to under `docs/guides/`

**Acceptance Criteria:**

- [ ] A Keystone CR with `spec.database.tls.enabled: true` causes the operator to create a cert-manager `Certificate` (via `tls.EnsureCertificate`), and a new condition (e.g. `DatabaseTLSReady`) progresses to `True` with a stable reason once the cert Secret is issued; with TLS unset/disabled the condition reports a `NotRequired`-style reason and no Certificate is created.
- [ ] The derived `<name>-db-connection` Secret's `connection` value contains `ssl_ca=`, `ssl_cert=`, `ssl_key=` query params (pointing at the in-pod mount path) when TLS is enabled, and is byte-identical to today's plaintext DSN when disabled (asserted by `reconcile_dbconnection_secret_test.go`).
- [ ] The `mode` enum maps to pymysql/SQLAlchemy connect args exactly: `prefer`/`require` → `ssl_ca`/`ssl_cert`/`ssl_key` only (encrypted, no peer/identity check); `verify-ca` → adds `ssl_verify_cert=true`; `verify-full` → adds `ssl_verify_cert=true&ssl_verify_identity=true`. The mapping is documented in `keystone-crd.md` and unit-tested.
- [ ] `buildKeystoneDeployment`, all five `buildDBJob` variants, and `buildBootstrapJob` mount the client-cert + CA volumes read-only at the documented path when TLS is enabled, and do not when disabled (name-based, not index-based, container/volume assertions per project pattern).
- [ ] The bootstrap inline `python3` pre-insert script connects with `pymysql.connect(..., ssl={...})` derived from the URL's `ssl_*` params (unit test renders `buildBootstrapJob` and asserts the script body wires the ssl dict).
- [ ] `make generate generate-common manifests sync-crds` produces no diff after the type change is committed (CI `verify-codegen`/`verify-crd-sync` green); both CRD YAML copies contain the `tls` schema and its CEL rule.
- [ ] The webhook `validate()` rejects an out-of-enum `mode` and the inconsistent `enabled`/secretRef combinations with `field.Invalid`, the CRD CEL rule rejects the identical inputs when the webhook is bypassed, and `TestValidateCreate_RunsAllValidations` exercises the new path.
- [ ] `cert-manager.io/certificates` verbs appear in both `keystone_controller.go` kubebuilder markers and `_helpers.tpl` `rbacRules`; `make manifests`/Helm RBAC tests confirm parity (no 403 path).
- [ ] `deploy/flux-system/infrastructure/mariadb.yaml` declares `spec.tls` with `required: true` and MaxScale TLS; the new cert-manager CA issuer resource is referenced in `deploy/flux-system/infrastructure/kustomization.yaml`; `hack/deploy-infra.sh` applies the DB CA before MariaDB becomes Ready.
- [ ] A new `tests/e2e/keystone/database-tls/chainsaw-test.yaml` deploys a TLS-enabled Keystone CR and asserts: (a) `SHOW STATUS LIKE 'Ssl_cipher'` against a Keystone pod returns a non-empty cipher; (b) with `tls.required: true`, a plaintext connection attempt is rejected. Catch blocks emit the full required diagnostic set (CR YAML, operator pod logs current + `--previous`, recent events, and MariaDB/MaxScale logs as the authoritative source for handshake failures).
- [ ] `docs/reference/keystone/keystone-crd.md` and `docs/reference/infrastructure/infrastructure-manifests.md` are updated in the same PR; doc tables match the actual webhook/CEL behavior and the new E2E directory is listed in the test inventory.

**Non-Goals:**

- **Connection pooling / SQLAlchemy `pool_*` tuning.** Out of scope; only the TLS connect args change. The issue listed connect_args as "to be specified" — they are specified above; pool sizing is unrelated.
- **mariadb-operator auto-generated CA vs. cert-manager-issued CA.** This plan recommends a cert-manager CA Issuer feeding `serverCASecretRef`/`clientCASecretRef`. Letting mariadb-operator self-generate its CAs and having the operator's client Certificate chain to it is a viable alternative but is deliberately not pursued, to keep one PKI root and satisfy "pin a cert-manager Issuer."
- **Cross-cluster / brownfield-host TLS automation.** Brownfield mode (`database.host` set, no `clusterRef`) may carry `tls.caBundleSecretRef`/`clientCertSecretRef` user-supplied, but the operator does not provision MariaDB-side TLS or issue certs for externally managed databases (per the issue's out-of-scope note).
- **OpenBao mTLS and Memcached TLS.** Tracked by the sibling issues referenced in the source issue; this PR only establishes the reusable pattern, it does not touch those paths.
- **Rotating/forcing TLS on existing CRs by default.** `spec.database.tls` is opt-in; `Default()` must not materialize `enabled: true` on upgrade (mirrors the established `TrustFlush`/`UWSGI` non-mutating-default discipline in `keystone_webhook.go`).
- **TLS for the operator↔kube-apiserver or webhook path.** Already handled by the existing `helm/.../templates/certificate.yaml`; unrelated.

**References:**

- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_dbconnection_secret.go` (DSN builder, lines 97-104; derived-Secret contract, header comment + line 41)
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_config.go:114-127, 308-327` (placeholder DSN; `resolveDatabaseHost`/`dbPort`)
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment.go:35-56, 144-262` (`buildDBConnectionEnvVar`, deployment volumes/mounts)
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_database.go:789-880` (`buildDBJob` and the five variants); `reconcile_bootstrap.go:~95-150` (inline pymysql pre-insert script)
- `internal/common/types/types.go:19-35` (`DatabaseSpec` canonical definition); `operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_types.go:57-62` (embed + CEL)
- `internal/common/tls/tls.go:28-88` (`EnsureCertificate`, `IsCertificateReady`, `GetTLSSecret`); `tls_test.go`, `internal/common/tls/integration_test.go`
- `operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_webhook.go:85-135, 176-244` (`Default`, `validate`, Database mutual-exclusivity precedent)
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller.go:160-179` (RBAC markers), `225-365` (sub-reconciler order), `589-640` (parallel group)
- `operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/templates/_helpers.tpl:66-185` (centralized `rbacRules`, CC-0043), `clusterrole.yaml`, `role.yaml`
- `deploy/flux-system/infrastructure/mariadb.yaml`, `cluster-issuer.yaml`, `openbao-tls-cert.yaml`, `kustomization.yaml`; `deploy/kind/infrastructure/kustomization.yaml`; `hack/deploy-infra.sh:989-1049`
- `Makefile:215-275` (`generate`, `generate-common`, `manifests`, `sync-crds`, `verify-crd-sync`)
- `tests/e2e/keystone/basic-deployment/`, `tests/e2e/keystone/brownfield-database/` (chainsaw conventions, CR fixtures, catch-block diagnostics)
- `docs/reference/keystone/keystone-crd.md:995-1010, 1120-1130` (DatabaseSpec + webhook tables); `docs/reference/infrastructure/infrastructure-manifests.md:43, 219-233`
- mariadb-operator `v0.38.1` `k8s.mariadb.com/v1alpha1` `spec.tls` (`serverCASecretRef`, `clientCASecretRef`, `required`, MaxScale TLS) and cert-manager `v1.17.1` `Certificate` — exact Go field names to be confirmed against the vendored module during implementation (could not be opened from this sandbox; YAML field names per the source issue)
- Source issue: https://github.com/C5C3/forge/issues/320; related review patterns: *Kubernetes TLS and Certificate Management*, *Secure by Design and Default*, *Sync RBAC markers with Helm ClusterRole templates*, *Ensure CRD schema validation mirrors webhook validation semantics*, *Regenerate auto-generated files after API type changes*

---

_Elaborated by [planwerk-review](https://github.com/planwerk/planwerk-review) with Claude Code_


**Labels:** enhancement

---
Source: https://github.com/C5C3/forge/issues/320

stories.title: Cluster operator enables encrypted Keystone->MariaDB traffic via spec.database.tls
stories.want: to set spec.database.tls.enabled: true (with mode) on a Keystone CR and have the operator provision the client certificate, wire the DSN, and mount the cert material automatically
stories.so_that: credentials and token-validation queries no longer traverse the pod network in cleartext, without any data-plane or schema changes
criteria: Setting spec.database.tls.enabled: true on a managed-mode (clusterRef) Keystone CR causes the operator to create a cert-manager Certificate named <keystone.Name>-db-client
The derived <keystone.Name>-db-connection Secret's connection value gains ssl_ca=, ssl_cert=, ssl_key= query params pointing at /etc/keystone/db-tls/
buildKeystoneDeployment, all five buildDBJob variants, and buildBootstrapJob mount the client-cert and CA volumes read-only at /etc/keystone/db-tls/
A new DatabaseTLSReady condition reaches status True with reason CertificateIssued once the cert Secret exists
Existing CRs with no spec.database.tls block produce a byte-identical plaintext DSN and zero Certificates (no behavior change)

stories.title: Security engineer enforces mutual-TLS with full peer/identity verification
stories.want: to choose a TLS verification mode (prefer | require | verify-ca | verify-full) and have it map deterministically to pymysql/SQLAlchemy connect args
stories.so_that: I can require the client to verify the MariaDB server CA and hostname (verify-full) and satisfy the project's Secure-by-Design review pattern
criteria: mode prefer and require add ssl_ca/ssl_cert/ssl_key only (encrypted, no peer/identity check)
mode verify-ca additionally adds ssl_verify_cert=true to the DSN RawQuery
mode verify-full additionally adds ssl_verify_cert=true&ssl_verify_identity=true
The mode->args mapping is implemented in one pure helper function with dedicated unit tests covering all four modes
An out-of-enum mode value is rejected by both the webhook and the CRD CEL rule before reaching the reconciler

stories.title: Operator developer reuses the DatabaseTLSSpec pattern for sibling operators
stories.want: DatabaseTLSSpec added to the shared internal/common/types.DatabaseSpec (not keystone-local) with regenerated deepcopy
stories.so_that: the OpenBao-mTLS and Memcached-TLS sibling work can copy a single canonical shape, consistent with how ClusterRef/Host/Port already live in the shared struct
criteria: DatabaseTLSSpec is defined in internal/common/types/types.go with Enabled bool, Mode string (enum marker), CABundleSecretRef and ClientCertSecretRef
DatabaseSpec gains a TLS *DatabaseTLSSpec optional pointer field
make generate-common regenerates internal/common/types/zz_generated.deepcopy.go with a DeepCopy for the new type and no manual edits
make generate regenerates operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/zz_generated.deepcopy.go consistently
go vet and golangci-lint pass on the changed packages

stories.title: Operator/admin receives precise admission errors for a misconfigured tls block
stories.want: the validating webhook and CRD schema to reject inconsistent spec.database.tls configurations with field-level messages
stories.so_that: I learn the exact misconfiguration at kubectl apply time instead of debugging a TLS handshake failure in pod logs
criteria: An out-of-enum mode is rejected with field.Invalid at spec.database.tls.mode by validate()
tls.enabled: true with a missing required secretRef is rejected with a field-level message
The CRD x-kubernetes-validations CEL rule rejects the identical inputs when the webhook is bypassed
TestValidateCreate_RunsAllValidations is extended to break the tls block and assert the new error path fires
Valid tls configurations (all four modes, enabled true/false) are accepted

stories.title: SRE observes DB-TLS readiness and failures through status conditions
stories.want: a dedicated DatabaseTLSReady condition with stable typed reasons reflecting certificate issuance state
stories.so_that: I can alert on TLS provisioning failures and distinguish them from generic DatabaseReady failures
criteria: DatabaseTLSReady is added to subConditionTypes and the subReconcilerConditionTypes drift map (CC-0089)
When tls is unset/disabled the condition reports status True with a NotRequired-style reason and no Certificate is created
When the Certificate exists but is not yet Ready the condition is False with reason CertificatePending and the reconcile requeues
When the Certificate is Ready the condition is True with reason CertificateIssued
Condition reason strings are extracted as typed constants, not inline string literals

stories.title: Infrastructure operator runs a TLS-enforcing MariaDB Galera + MaxScale cluster
stories.want: deploy/flux-system/infrastructure/mariadb.yaml to declare spec.tls (required: true) plus MaxScale TLS, fed by a dedicated cert-manager CA Issuer applied before MariaDB becomes Ready
stories.so_that: the server side actually rejects plaintext and the Keystone client cert chains to a trusted CA across the MaxScale proxy hop
criteria: mariadb.yaml gains spec.tls with enabled: true, required: true, serverCASecretRef, clientCASecretRef, and MaxScale TLS
A new deploy/flux-system/infrastructure/db-ca-issuer.yaml defines a cert-manager CA Issuer/Certificate (a CA keypair, not the leaf selfsigned-cluster-issuer) and is listed in kustomization.yaml
hack/deploy-infra.sh applies the DB CA in Phase 2 before MariaDB becomes Ready, mirroring the openbao-tls-cert.yaml ordering
deploy/kind/infrastructure/kustomization.yaml keeps the MariaDB strategic-merge patch TLS-consistent (or documents the divergence)
kubectl apply -k deploy/kind/infrastructure renders without error

stories.title: QA verifies end-to-end encryption and plaintext rejection
stories.want: a chainsaw E2E suite that deploys a TLS-enabled Keystone CR and proves the live connection is encrypted and plaintext is refused
stories.so_that: regressions in the TLS path are caught in CI before release
criteria: tests/e2e/keystone/database-tls/chainsaw-test.yaml deploys a TLS-enabled Keystone CR and waits for Ready
It asserts SHOW STATUS LIKE 'Ssl_cipher' against a Keystone pod returns a non-empty cipher
With tls.required: true it asserts a plaintext connection attempt is rejected
Catch blocks emit the full diagnostic set: CR YAML, operator pod logs (current + --previous), recent events, and MariaDB/MaxScale logs
The new directory is added to the keystone-crd.md E2E inventory

stories.title: Documentation reader learns how to enable DB TLS and what the modes mean
stories.want: updated reference tables for DatabaseTLSSpec/webhook/CEL and a how-to guide for enabling Keystone DB TLS
stories.so_that: I can configure mTLS correctly without reading the operator source
criteria: docs/reference/keystone/keystone-crd.md gains a DatabaseTLSSpec field table and a mode->connect-args mapping table matching the implemented behavior
The webhook-validation and CEL tables in keystone-crd.md list the new tls rules
docs/reference/infrastructure/infrastructure-manifests.md documents MariaDB spec.tls and the new db-ca-issuer.yaml
A new docs/guides/ how-to follows the Prerequisites -> Steps -> Verification -> See Also structure
The E2E inventory in keystone-crd.md lists tests/e2e/keystone/database-tls/

requirements.description: The system SHALL define DatabaseTLSSpec in internal/common/types and add an optional TLS *DatabaseTLSSpec pointer to the shared DatabaseSpec, with regenerated deepcopy for both internal/common/types and the keystone api package.
requirements.rationale: Single canonical shape reusable by sibling operators; pointer keeps the field opt-in and non-mutating; deepcopy regen is a project BLOCKING pattern.

scenarios.name: Type compiles with markers
scenarios.when: DatabaseTLSSpec is added with kubebuilder enum marker on Mode and optional markers
scenarios.then: go build of internal/common/types and operators/keystone succeeds
and_then: go vet passes

scenarios.name: Deepcopy regenerated
scenarios.when: make generate generate-common is run after the type change
scenarios.then: zz_generated.deepcopy.go in both internal/common/types and api/v1alpha1 contains a DeepCopy/DeepCopyInto for DatabaseTLSSpec
and_then: a second run produces no further diff

scenarios.name: Nil TLS preserves behavior
scenarios.then: DeepCopy yields TLS == nil and all existing DatabaseSpec consumers compile unchanged
and_then: no existing test referencing DatabaseSpec breaks for an unrelated reason

requirements.description: The operator SHALL, when spec.database.tls.enabled is true and the CR is managed-mode, issue a cert-manager Certificate for the Keystone DB client keypair via tls.EnsureCertificate, gated by a new DatabaseTLSReady condition with typed reasons.
requirements.rationale: First production consumer of the already-tested tls package; condition gates downstream pods that mount the cert.

scenarios.name: Certificate created when enabled
scenarios.when: reconcile runs with tls.enabled true and clusterRef set
scenarios.then: a cert-manager Certificate <name>-db-client is created with an owner reference to the Keystone CR
and_then: DatabaseTLSReady is False with reason CertificatePending and the reconcile requeues until the Secret is issued

scenarios.name: Condition True when issued
scenarios.when: the Certificate has a Ready=True condition
scenarios.then: DatabaseTLSReady is set True with reason CertificateIssued
and_then: downstream sub-reconcilers proceed

scenarios.name: Disabled is a no-op
scenarios.when: tls is nil or enabled is false
scenarios.then: no Certificate is created and DatabaseTLSReady is True with reason NotRequired
and_then: the reconcile does not requeue on account of TLS

scenarios.name: Brownfield does not provision
scenarios.when: tls.enabled true but the CR is brownfield (host set, no clusterRef)
scenarios.then: the operator does not create a Certificate and relies on user-supplied secret refs
and_then: DatabaseTLSReady reports a reason indicating externally managed certs

requirements.description: reconcileDBConnectionSecret SHALL append ssl_ca, ssl_cert, ssl_key (and mode-dependent ssl_verify_cert/ssl_verify_identity) to connURL.RawQuery pointing at the in-pod mount path when TLS is enabled, and SHALL produce a byte-identical plaintext DSN when TLS is disabled.
requirements.rationale: CC-0080 made the derived-Secret DSN builder the single cleanest injection point; the operator references file paths, never reads the keypair.

scenarios.name: ssl params appended when enabled
scenarios.when: the derived Secret is built with tls.enabled true
scenarios.then: the connection value RawQuery contains charset=utf8 plus ssl_ca=/etc/keystone/db-tls/ca.crt, ssl_cert=/etc/keystone/db-tls/tls.crt, ssl_key=/etc/keystone/db-tls/tls.key
and_then: the operator does not read tls.crt/tls.key bytes

scenarios.name: Byte-identical when disabled
scenarios.then: connStr equals the pre-feature value mysql+pymysql://user:pass@host/db?charset=utf8 exactly
and_then: the existing exact-string assertion in reconcile_dbconnection_secret_test.go still passes after update

scenarios.name: Mode reflected in query
scenarios.then: RawQuery additionally contains ssl_verify_cert=true and ssl_verify_identity=true
and_then: query param ordering is deterministic across reconciles

requirements.description: The system SHALL map the mode enum to connect args exactly: prefer/require -> ssl_ca+ssl_cert+ssl_key only; verify-ca -> add ssl_verify_cert=true; verify-full -> add ssl_verify_cert=true & ssl_verify_identity=true.
requirements.rationale: Deterministic, documented, unit-tested mapping prevents silent downgrade of verification strength.

scenarios.name: prefer/require
scenarios.then: only ssl_ca/ssl_cert/ssl_key are emitted
and_then: no ssl_verify_* params present

scenarios.name: verify-ca
scenarios.then: ssl_verify_cert=true is added and ssl_verify_identity is absent

scenarios.name: verify-full
scenarios.then: both ssl_verify_cert=true and ssl_verify_identity=true are added

scenarios.name: unknown mode
scenarios.when: mode is an out-of-enum value reaching the mapper
scenarios.then: the mapper returns an error or the value is already rejected upstream by validation
and_then: no partially-formed DSN is written

requirements.description: buildKeystoneDeployment, all five buildDBJob variants, and buildBootstrapJob SHALL mount the client-cert Secret and the server-CA as read-only volumes at /etc/keystone/db-tls/ when TLS is enabled, and SHALL NOT mount them when disabled.
requirements.rationale: Every Keystone-owned workload that opens a MySQL connection needs the cert material at a fixed path; assertions must be name-based per project pattern.

scenarios.name: Deployment mounts when enabled
scenarios.when: buildKeystoneDeployment is rendered with tls.enabled true
scenarios.then: the pod has a read-only volume+mount named db-tls at /etc/keystone/db-tls/ sourced from the cert Secret
and_then: existing config/fernet-keys/credential-keys mounts are unchanged

scenarios.name: All five db_sync variants mount
scenarios.then: each Job pod mounts the db-tls volume read-only at the documented path
and_then: the shared buildDBJob change covers all five

scenarios.name: No mount when disabled
scenarios.then: no db-tls volume or mount is present in any builder output
and_then: builder output is byte-equivalent to pre-feature for the disabled path

requirements.description: The bootstrap inline python3 pre-insert script SHALL translate the URL's ssl_* query params into a pymysql ssl= dict, since it hand-parses the URL and pymysql.connect takes ssl= (a dict), not ssl_ca=.
requirements.rationale: Bootstrap bypasses SQLAlchemy; without translation the pre-insert connect would be plaintext and fail against tls.required.

scenarios.name: ssl dict built when params present
scenarios.then: the script builds ssl={'ca':...,'cert':...,'key':...} and passes it to pymysql.connect
and_then: check_hostname/verification reflects verify-ca/verify-full params

scenarios.name: No ssl when params absent
scenarios.when: the URL has no ssl_* params
scenarios.then: pymysql.connect is called without an ssl kwarg (plaintext, unchanged behavior)

scenarios.name: Rendered script asserted
scenarios.when: buildBootstrapJob is unit-rendered with TLS enabled
scenarios.then: the script body contains the ssl-dict construction wired into pymysql.connect
and_then: the keystone-manage bootstrap step is unchanged

requirements.description: The webhook validate() SHALL reject an out-of-enum mode and inconsistent enabled/secretRef combinations with field.Invalid, and an equivalent CRD x-kubernetes-validations CEL rule SHALL reject the identical inputs when the webhook is bypassed.
requirements.rationale: Project BLOCKING patterns: defense-in-depth on similarly-marked fields and CRD schema mirroring webhook semantics (CC-0011/CC-0039).

scenarios.name: Out-of-enum mode rejected
scenarios.then: validate() appends field.Invalid at spec.database.tls.mode and admission is denied
and_then: the CRD CEL rule denies the same input via kubectl with the webhook disabled

scenarios.name: Enabled without required secretRef rejected
scenarios.when: tls.enabled true but a required secretRef is empty
scenarios.then: validate() returns a field-level error naming the missing ref
and_then: the CEL rule denies the equivalent object

scenarios.name: Valid configs accepted
scenarios.when: tls.enabled true with all required refs and a valid mode, or tls disabled/absent
scenarios.then: validate() returns nil and admission succeeds
and_then: TestValidateCreate_RunsAllValidations exercises the new failing path

requirements.description: cert-manager.io certificates verbs (get;list;watch;create;update;patch;delete) SHALL be added to both the keystone_controller.go kubebuilder RBAC markers and the centralized _helpers.tpl rbacRules in lockstep, with Helm RBAC parity tests updated.
requirements.rationale: Project BLOCKING pattern: a marker-only change ships a 403 at runtime (CC-0037/CC-0038/CC-0043).

scenarios.name: Marker added
scenarios.when: the kubebuilder RBAC block is updated
scenarios.then: make manifests regenerates the ClusterRole/Role config with the cert-manager.io rule
and_then: the rule grants the full CRUD verb set on certificates

scenarios.name: Helm rule added
scenarios.then: clusterrole_test.yaml and role_test.yaml rule-count and content assertions are updated to match
and_then: helm unittest passes with the new expected rule count

scenarios.name: Parity holds
scenarios.when: ClusterRole and Role are both rendered
scenarios.then: both contain the identical cert-manager.io rule (CC-0043 sync)

requirements.description: deploy/flux-system/infrastructure/mariadb.yaml SHALL declare spec.tls (enabled, required: true, serverCASecretRef, clientCASecretRef) plus MaxScale TLS; a new db-ca-issuer.yaml cert-manager CA resource SHALL be added to kustomization.yaml; hack/deploy-infra.sh SHALL apply the DB CA before MariaDB becomes Ready; the kind overlay SHALL stay TLS-consistent or document the divergence.
requirements.rationale: Server-side enforcement and a single PKI root are required for the client cert to be trusted across the MaxScale hop; deploy ordering mirrors the openbao-tls precedent.

scenarios.name: MariaDB TLS declared
scenarios.then: spec.tls has enabled true, required true, serverCASecretRef, clientCASecretRef and MaxScale TLS set
and_then: YAML field names follow mariadb-operator v0.38.1 k8s.mariadb.com/v1alpha1 (Go field names confirmed against the module during implementation)

scenarios.name: CA issuer ordered before MariaDB
scenarios.then: db-ca-issuer.yaml is applied alongside cluster-issuer.yaml/openbao-tls-cert.yaml before MariaDB Ready
and_then: kustomization.yaml lists db-ca-issuer.yaml

scenarios.name: Kind overlay renders
scenarios.then: the MariaDB strategic-merge patch keeps TLS consistent (or an in-file comment documents the intentional divergence)
and_then: no kustomize build error

requirements.description: After the API type change is committed, make generate generate-common manifests sync-crds SHALL produce no diff, and both CRD YAML copies SHALL contain the tls schema and its CEL rule (CI verify-codegen/verify-crd-sync green).
requirements.rationale: Project BLOCKING pattern: regenerate auto-generated files after API type changes; two CRD copies must stay synced.

scenarios.name: Codegen idempotent
scenarios.when: the four make targets are run twice
scenarios.then: the second run yields no git diff
and_then: zz_generated files and CRD YAMLs are committed

scenarios.name: Both CRDs carry tls schema
scenarios.when: config/crd/bases and helm/.../crds copies are inspected
scenarios.then: both contain the database.tls properties and the new x-kubernetes-validations CEL rule
and_then: make verify-crd-sync exits 0

scenarios.name: CEL present on database
scenarios.then: the existing clusterRef/host XOR rule is preserved and the new tls rule is added

requirements.description: A new tests/e2e/keystone/database-tls/ chainsaw suite SHALL deploy a TLS-enabled Keystone CR and assert (a) SHOW STATUS LIKE 'Ssl_cipher' returns a non-empty cipher from a Keystone pod and (b) with tls.required true a plaintext connection is rejected, with full catch-block diagnostics.
requirements.rationale: 1-feature-to-1-E2E-dir convention; MariaDB/MaxScale logs are the authoritative source for handshake failures.

scenarios.name: Encrypted connection proven
scenarios.when: the TLS Keystone CR is deployed and Ready
scenarios.then: Ssl_cipher queried via a Keystone pod is non-empty
and_then: the assertion fails the test if the cipher is empty

scenarios.name: Plaintext rejected
scenarios.when: a plaintext connection is attempted against the tls.required MariaDB
scenarios.then: the connection is refused
and_then: the negative assertion passes only on rejection

scenarios.name: Diagnostics on failure
scenarios.then: catch emits CR YAML, operator logs current + --previous, recent events, and MariaDB/MaxScale logs
and_then: a single CI run is sufficient to triage a handshake failure

requirements.description: docs/reference/keystone/keystone-crd.md and docs/reference/infrastructure/infrastructure-manifests.md SHALL be updated in the same change, and a new docs/guides/ how-to SHALL be added; doc tables SHALL match the actual webhook/CEL behavior and list the new E2E directory.
requirements.rationale: Diataxis reference + how-to required for a new user-facing CRD sub-spec; docs must not drift from validation behavior.

scenarios.name: Reference updated
scenarios.then: it has a DatabaseTLSSpec field table, a mode->connect-args table, updated webhook/CEL tables, and the new E2E dir in the inventory
and_then: tables match implemented enum and messages verbatim

scenarios.name: Infra doc updated
scenarios.then: it documents MariaDB spec.tls and db-ca-issuer.yaml

scenarios.name: How-to added
scenarios.then: it follows Prerequisites -> Steps -> Verification -> See Also and links to the reference

requirements.description: Default() SHALL NOT materialize spec.database.tls.enabled: true (opt-in, non-mutating default) and SHALL only default mode to a documented value when tls is non-nil and mode is empty.
requirements.rationale: Mirrors established TrustFlush/UWSGI non-mutating-default discipline; an upgrade must not silently turn on TLS.

scenarios.name: No TLS on upgrade
scenarios.when: an existing CR with no tls block passes through Default()
scenarios.then: spec.database.tls remains nil
and_then: no Certificate is created for previously-plaintext CRs

scenarios.name: Mode defaulted only when block present
scenarios.when: tls is non-nil with empty mode
scenarios.then: mode is defaulted to the documented baseline (e.g. require) and enabled is left untouched
and_then: enabled:false with a defaulted mode still creates no Certificate

scenarios.name: Explicit values preserved
scenarios.then: Default() does not overwrite it

requirements.description: The DatabaseTLSReady sub-reconciler SHALL be inserted in dependency order after Secrets and before DBConnectionSecret/Config/Deployment, and registered in subConditionTypes and the subReconcilerConditionTypes drift map.
requirements.rationale: The cert Secret must exist before the DSN references its mount path and before pods/Jobs mount it; CC-0089 requires drift-map registration.

scenarios.name: Ordered before DSN
scenarios.then: reconcileDatabaseTLS executes after reconcileSecrets and before reconcileDBConnectionSecret
and_then: DBConnectionSecret can safely reference the cert mount path

scenarios.name: Drift map registered
scenarios.then: DatabaseTLS -> DatabaseTLSReady is present and DatabaseTLSReady is in subConditionTypes
and_then: the Ready aggregate accounts for the new condition

scenarios.name: Disabled fast-path
scenarios.then: the sub-reconciler returns a zero result with the NotRequired reason without blocking the loop

tasks.title: Add DatabaseTLSSpec (Enabled bool; Mode string with +kubebuilder:validation:Enum=prefer;require;verify-ca;verify-full and +optional; CABundleSecretRef SecretRefSpec; ClientCertSecretRef SecretRefSpec) and TLS *DatabaseTLSSpec optional pointer on DatabaseSpec in internal/common/types/types.go. Document field semantics in Go doc comments (REQ-001).

Run make generate generate-common; commit regenerated internal/common/types/zz_generated.deepcopy.go and operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/zz_generated.deepcopy.go. Add TestDatabaseTLSSpec_DeepCopy and TestDatabaseSpec_TLSField_OptionalPointer unit tests asserting independent deep copies and nil->nil (REQ-001).

Create dbtls_mode.go with a pure helper modeToSSLParams(mode, basePaths) returning ordered ssl_* query params per the exact mapping; add typed condition-reason constants (conditionTypeDatabaseTLSReady, reasonCertificateIssued/CertificatePending/NotRequired/ExternallyManaged) in the controller package. Add dbtls_mode_test.go covering all four modes + deterministic ordering + unknown-mode error (REQ-004).

Add CRD CEL +kubebuilder:validation:XValidation rule(s) for the tls sub-block on KeystoneSpec.Database in keystone_types.go (preserve the existing clusterRef/host XOR rule): reject out-of-enum mode and enabled-without-required-secretRef. Update the Database doc comment (REQ-007, REQ-010).

Add defense-in-depth validation in keystone_webhook.go validate(): field.Invalid at spec.database.tls.mode for out-of-enum mode, and field-level error for enabled-without-required-secretRef, mirroring the existing Database mutual-exclusivity block semantics (REQ-007).

Add non-mutating Default() handling in keystone_webhook.go: never set tls or enabled; only default mode to the documented baseline when tls != nil and mode == "". Mirror the TrustFlush/UWSGI non-mutating discipline (REQ-013).

Add webhook unit tests: TestValidate_RejectsOutOfEnumTLSMode, TestValidate_RejectsEnabledWithoutSecretRef, TestDefault_DoesNotMaterializeTLSEnabled, TestDefault_DefaultsModeOnlyWhenBlockPresent; extend TestValidateCreate_RunsAllValidations to break the tls block and assert the new error (REQ-007, REQ-013).

Run make manifests sync-crds; commit both CRD copies (config/crd/bases + helm/.../crds) carrying the database.tls schema and new CEL rule. Verify make verify-crd-sync exits 0 and a second codegen run is diff-free (REQ-010).

Add CRD CEL integration test TestIntegration_CRD_CELRejectsInvalidTLS in api/v1alpha1/integration_test.go: under envtest with the webhook disabled, assert the CRD rejects out-of-enum mode and inconsistent enabled+secretRef (REQ-007).

Create operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_databasetls.go: reconcileDatabaseTLS builds a cert-manager Certificate (issuerRef = DB CA issuer, secretName = <name>-db-client) and calls tls.EnsureCertificate; sets DatabaseTLSReady via typed reasons; disabled/brownfield fast-paths to NotRequired/ExternallyManaged (REQ-002, REQ-014).

Wire reconcileDatabaseTLS into keystone_controller.go via instrumentSubReconciler AFTER Secrets and BEFORE DBConnectionSecret; add DatabaseTLSReady to subConditionTypes and subReconcilerConditionTypes drift map (CC-0089); add the +kubebuilder:rbac cert-manager.io certificates marker (get;list;watch;create;update;patch;delete) at lines ~160-179 (REQ-008, REQ-014).

Add reconcile_databasetls_test.go: TestReconcileDatabaseTLS_CreatesCertificateWhenEnabled, _ConditionTrueWhenIssued, _DisabledIsNoOp, plus a brownfield ExternallyManaged case. Add TestReconcile_DatabaseTLSOrderedBeforeDBConnectionSecret to keystone_controller_test.go (REQ-002, REQ-014).

Add the cert-manager.io certificates rule to keystone-operator.rbacRules in _helpers.tpl; update tests/clusterrole_test.yaml and tests/role_test.yaml rule-count and content assertions for the new rule and CC-0043 parity (REQ-008).

Add envtest integration test TestIntegration_DatabaseTLS_CertificateLifecycle in internal/controller/integration_test.go: applying a TLS-enabled Keystone CR creates and owns a cert-manager Certificate and progresses DatabaseTLSReady (REQ-002).

Modify reconcile_dbconnection_secret.go: when keystone.Spec.Database.TLS != nil && Enabled, append ssl_ca/ssl_cert/ssl_key (mount path /etc/keystone/db-tls/) plus mode-dependent params via modeToSSLParams to connURL.RawQuery; never read keypair bytes. Update reconcile_dbconnection_secret_test.go: TestDBConnectionSecret_AppendsSSLParamsWhenEnabled and _ByteIdenticalWhenDisabled (fix the existing exact-string assertion) (REQ-003, REQ-004).

Add a shared db-tls volume/volumeMount helper and apply it in buildKeystoneDeployment (reconcile_deployment.go) and buildDBJob (reconcile_database.go, covering all five variants): read-only volume named db-tls at /etc/keystone/db-tls/ from the <name>-db-client Secret, only when TLS enabled. Add name-based assertions in reconcile_deployment_test.go and reconcile_database_test.go (REQ-005).

Modify buildBootstrapJob (reconcile_bootstrap.go): mount the db-tls volume read-only, and extend the inline python3 pre-insert script to parse ssl_* query params into a pymysql ssl={...} dict (with verify-ca/verify-full honoring cert/identity verification) passed to pymysql.connect; no ssl kwarg when absent. Add TestBuildBootstrapJob_ScriptWiresSSLDict and _NoSSLKwargWhenDisabled (REQ-005, REQ-006).

Create deploy/flux-system/infrastructure/db-ca-issuer.yaml: a cert-manager CA Issuer/ClusterIssuer (CA keypair, NOT the leaf selfsigned-cluster-issuer) plus the CA Certificate(s) backing serverCASecretRef/clientCASecretRef. Add the new file to deploy/flux-system/infrastructure/kustomization.yaml resources (REQ-009).

Edit deploy/flux-system/infrastructure/mariadb.yaml: add spec.tls (enabled, required: true, serverCASecretRef, clientCASecretRef) and MaxScale TLS per mariadb-operator v0.38.1 k8s.mariadb.com/v1alpha1. Confirm exact Go/YAML field names against the vendored module; leave a # DECISION comment if a name is inferred (REQ-009).

Update deploy/kind/infrastructure/kustomization.yaml MariaDB strategic-merge patch to keep TLS consistent (single-replica, no Galera/MaxScale path) OR add an in-file comment documenting the intentional divergence. Verify kubectl apply -k deploy/kind/infrastructure --dry-run=client succeeds (REQ-009).

Edit hack/deploy-infra.sh Phase 2 to kubectl apply db-ca-issuer.yaml alongside cluster-issuer.yaml/openbao-tls-cert.yaml so the DB CA Secret exists before MariaDB becomes Ready (mirror lines ~997-999) (REQ-009).

Create tests/e2e/keystone/database-tls/00-keystone-cr.yaml (managed-mode CR with spec.database.tls.enabled true, mode verify-full) and any required Secret fixtures, following the basic-deployment fixture conventions (REQ-011).

Create tests/e2e/keystone/database-tls/chainsaw-test.yaml: deploy the CR, wait Ready, assert non-empty Ssl_cipher via SHOW STATUS from a Keystone pod, assert plaintext connection rejected under tls.required; catch blocks emit CR YAML, operator logs current + --previous, recent events, and MariaDB/MaxScale logs (REQ-011).

Update docs/reference/keystone/keystone-crd.md: add DatabaseTLSSpec field table, mode->connect-args mapping table, update the webhook-validation and CEL tables for the new tls rules, and add tests/e2e/keystone/database-tls/ to the E2E inventory (REQ-012).

Update docs/reference/infrastructure/infrastructure-manifests.md (MariaDB spec.tls + db-ca-issuer.yaml) and add a new docs/guides/ how-to (Prerequisites -> Steps -> Verification -> See Also) for enabling Keystone DB TLS/mTLS (REQ-012).

test_specifications.story: Operator developer reuses the DatabaseTLSSpec pattern for sibling operators
test_specifications.expected: DeepCopy of a populated DatabaseTLSSpec returns an independent equal value; nil TLS deep-copies to nil

test_specifications.story: Operator developer reuses the DatabaseTLSSpec pattern for sibling operators
test_specifications.expected: A DatabaseSpec with TLS nil and one with TLS set both round-trip through DeepCopy without aliasing

test_specifications.story: Cluster operator enables encrypted Keystone->MariaDB traffic via spec.database.tls
test_specifications.expected: With tls.enabled true a cert-manager Certificate is created with owner ref and DatabaseTLSReady is False/CertificatePending

test_specifications.story: SRE observes DB-TLS readiness and failures through status conditions
test_specifications.expected: When the Certificate is Ready=True, DatabaseTLSReady becomes True/CertificateIssued

test_specifications.story: Cluster operator enables encrypted Keystone->MariaDB traffic via spec.database.tls
test_specifications.expected: tls nil/disabled creates no Certificate and sets DatabaseTLSReady True/NotRequired

test_specifications.story: Cluster operator enables encrypted Keystone->MariaDB traffic via spec.database.tls
test_specifications.expected: Under envtest, applying a TLS-enabled Keystone CR creates and owns a cert-manager Certificate and progresses DatabaseTLSReady

test_specifications.story: Cluster operator enables encrypted Keystone->MariaDB traffic via spec.database.tls
test_specifications.expected: connection value RawQuery contains ssl_ca/ssl_cert/ssl_key at /etc/keystone/db-tls/ when TLS enabled

test_specifications.story: Cluster operator enables encrypted Keystone->MariaDB traffic via spec.database.tls
test_specifications.expected: Disabled path yields exactly mysql+pymysql://...?charset=utf8 (pre-feature string)

test_specifications.story: Security engineer enforces mutual-TLS with full peer/identity verification
test_specifications.expected: prefer/require -> ssl_ca/cert/key only; verify-ca adds ssl_verify_cert=true; verify-full adds both verify params

test_specifications.story: Security engineer enforces mutual-TLS with full peer/identity verification
test_specifications.expected: Repeated calls produce identical query-param ordering

test_specifications.story: Cluster operator enables encrypted Keystone->MariaDB traffic via spec.database.tls
test_specifications.expected: Name-based assertion that a read-only db-tls volume+mount at /etc/keystone/db-tls/ exists when enabled, absent when disabled

test_specifications.story: Cluster operator enables encrypted Keystone->MariaDB traffic via spec.database.tls
test_specifications.expected: db-sync/expand/migrate/contract/schema-check Jobs each mount the db-tls volume read-only when enabled

test_specifications.story: Cluster operator enables encrypted Keystone->MariaDB traffic via spec.database.tls
test_specifications.expected: Rendered python3 script builds an ssl={...} dict from ssl_* params and passes it to pymysql.connect when TLS enabled

test_specifications.story: Cluster operator enables encrypted Keystone->MariaDB traffic via spec.database.tls
test_specifications.expected: Disabled path renders pymysql.connect with no ssl kwarg (unchanged behavior)

test_specifications.story: Operator/admin receives precise admission errors for a misconfigured tls block
test_specifications.expected: validate() returns field.Invalid at spec.database.tls.mode for a bogus mode

test_specifications.story: Operator/admin receives precise admission errors for a misconfigured tls block
test_specifications.expected: validate() returns a field-level error when tls.enabled true but a required secretRef is empty

test_specifications.story: Operator/admin receives precise admission errors for a misconfigured tls block
test_specifications.expected: The aggregate test breaks the tls block and asserts the new error is among the returned errors

test_specifications.story: Operator/admin receives precise admission errors for a misconfigured tls block
test_specifications.expected: Under envtest with the webhook disabled, the CRD CEL rule rejects an out-of-enum mode / inconsistent enabled+secretRef

test_specifications.test_function: should grant cert-manager.io certificates CRUD + updated rule count [unit]
test_specifications.story: Cluster operator enables encrypted Keystone->MariaDB traffic via spec.database.tls
test_specifications.expected: Rendered ClusterRole contains the cert-manager.io certificates rule and the rule-count assertion is updated

test_specifications.test_function: should match ClusterRole including cert-manager.io rule [unit]
test_specifications.story: Cluster operator enables encrypted Keystone->MariaDB traffic via spec.database.tls

test_specifications.story: SRE observes DB-TLS readiness and failures through status conditions

test_specifications.story: Operator/admin receives precise admission errors for a misconfigured tls block

test_specifications.story: Security engineer enforces mutual-TLS with full peer/identity verification

review_criteria: BLOCKER: make generate generate-common manifests sync-crds is diff-free after commit; both CRD copies contain the database.tls schema and CEL rule; make verify-crd-sync exits 0 (REQ-010)

BLOCKER: cert-manager.io/certificates verbs present in BOTH keystone_controller.go kubebuilder markers AND _helpers.tpl rbacRules; helm clusterrole_test.yaml/role_test.yaml rule-count + content updated and passing (REQ-008)

BLOCKER: derived <name>-db-connection connection value is byte-identical to the pre-feature plaintext DSN when TLS is disabled, and contains ssl_ca/ssl_cert/ssl_key (+mode params) at /etc/keystone/db-tls/ when enabled; operator never reads keypair bytes (REQ-003)
BLOCKER: webhook validate() and CRD CEL reject the identical invalid tls inputs (out-of-enum mode; enabled-without-required-secretRef); TestValidateCreate_RunsAllValidations extended (REQ-007)

CRITICAL: mode->connect-args mapping is exactly prefer/require=ssl_ca/cert/key only, verify-ca=+ssl_verify_cert, verify-full=+ssl_verify_cert&ssl_verify_identity, unit-tested with deterministic ordering (REQ-004)

CRITICAL: buildKeystoneDeployment, all five buildDBJob variants, and buildBootstrapJob mount the read-only db-tls volume at the documented path only when enabled, asserted name-based not index-based (REQ-005)
CRITICAL: bootstrap inline python3 script wires ssl_* into a pymysql ssl= dict (honoring verify-ca/verify-full) and omits it when disabled (REQ-006)
CRITICAL: Default() never materializes tls/enabled on upgrade; only defaults mode when the block is present (non-mutating discipline) (REQ-013)

CRITICAL: reconcileDatabaseTLS runs after Secrets and before DBConnectionSecret; DatabaseTLSReady is in subConditionTypes and the CC-0089 drift map; condition reasons are typed constants (REQ-002, REQ-014)

MAJOR: mariadb.yaml declares spec.tls required:true + MaxScale TLS; db-ca-issuer.yaml is a CA issuer (not the leaf selfsigned issuer) referenced in kustomization.yaml; deploy-infra.sh applies the DB CA before MariaDB Ready; kind overlay TLS-consistent or divergence documented (REQ-009)
MAJOR: tests/e2e/keystone/database-tls/chainsaw-test.yaml asserts non-empty Ssl_cipher and plaintext rejection with the full catch-block diagnostic set including MariaDB/MaxScale logs (REQ-011)

MINOR: keystone-crd.md and infrastructure-manifests.md updated in the same change; doc tables match implemented enum/messages verbatim; new how-to follows Diataxis structure; E2E dir listed in inventory (REQ-012)
MINOR: go vet and golangci-lint clean on all changed Go packages; no type:ignore-equivalent suppressions; tests distributed across phases (not deferred)
implementation_notes:
ARCHITECTURE: This is a Go/Kubernetes operator feature; there is NO frontend layer, so the fullstack frontend checklist is intentionally not applied. The data plane (pymysql/SQLAlchemy/keystone-manage) is unchanged - only the DSN query string, mounted cert material, and MariaDB/MaxScale posture change.

CANONICAL FACTS (verified): DatabaseSpec lives ONLY in internal/common/types/types.go:19-35 (no keystone-local mirror); keystone embeds it as commonv1.DatabaseSpec at keystone_types.go:62 with the existing CEL `has(self.clusterRef) != has(self.host)` at line 61 (must be preserved). The DSN builder is reconcile_dbconnection_secret.go:97-104 (url.URL with RawQuery="charset=utf8"); dbConnectionSecretKey="connection" at line 41; the placeholder `mysql+pymysql://placeholder` in reconcile_config.go:53-60 is UNCHANGED. tls.EnsureCertificate/IsCertificateReady/GetTLSSecret in internal/common/tls/tls.go are fully tested with ZERO production call sites - this is the first consumer; cert-manager API is certmanagerv1 "github.com/cert-manager/cert-manager/pkg/apis/certmanager/v1" (cert-manager v1.17.1). mariadb-operator is v0.38.1; its Go TLS field names could NOT be confirmed in this sandbox (module not in cache, no network) - task 5.2 MUST confirm them against the vendored module and leave a # DECISION comment if inferred.

KEY PATTERNS TO FOLLOW: condition reasons as typed constants like reconcile_database.go:78-99; sub-reconciler wiring via instrumentSubReconciler with subConditionTypes (keystone_controller.go:103-117) + subReconcilerConditionTypes drift map (instrumentation.go:54-69, CC-0089); webhook defense-in-depth mirroring CEL (keystone_webhook.go:237-245); centralized Helm RBAC via _helpers.tpl rbacRules included by clusterrole.yaml AND role.yaml (CC-0043) with helm-unittest rule-count assertions (currently 21 -> 22); deploy ordering precedent hack/deploy-infra.sh Phase 2 lines ~997-999; chainsaw catch-block diagnostics from tests/e2e/keystone/basic-deployment & brownfield-database.

PITFALLS: (1) reconcile_dbconnection_secret_test.go has exact-string DSN assertions that WILL break - expected, update them. (2) buildDBJob is shared by all five db_sync variants - one change covers all; assert each variant. (3) bootstrap script hand-parses the URL and pymysql.connect needs ssl= dict, not ssl_ca= - translation is mandatory or bootstrap fails under tls.required. (4) Two CRD YAML copies must stay synced via make sync-crds. (5) helm rule-count assertions in BOTH clusterrole_test.yaml and role_test.yaml must be bumped together. (6) Default() must NOT set enabled - mirrors TrustFlush/UWSGI bool carve-out. (7) RBAC marker-only change ships a 403 - markers AND _helpers.tpl in lockstep.

DATA FLOW (TLS enabled, managed mode):
  Keystone CR (spec.database.tls.enabled=true, mode)
    -> reconcileSecrets (upstream creds synced by ESO)
    -> reconcileDatabaseTLS: tls.EnsureCertificate -> cert-manager Certificate <name>-db-client -> Secret (tls.crt/tls.key/ca.crt); DatabaseTLSReady
    -> reconcileDBConnectionSecret: connURL.RawQuery += ssl_ca=/etc/keystone/db-tls/ca.crt&ssl_cert=...&ssl_key=...[+ssl_verify_*] ; derived <name>-db-connection Secret
    -> reconcileConfig (placeholder ConfigMap, unchanged)
    -> buildKeystoneDeployment / buildDBJob x5 / buildBootstrapJob: mount db-tls volume RO at /etc/keystone/db-tls/ ; OS_DATABASE__CONNECTION env from derived Secret
    -> Pod: SQLAlchemy/pymysql opens TLS conn to MaxScale -> Galera (tls.required rejects plaintext)

ASCII ARCHITECTURE:
  +-------------------+      owns       +-----------------------+
  | Keystone CR       |---------------->| cert-manager          |
  | spec.database.tls |  EnsureCert     | Certificate           |
  +---------+---------+                 | <name>-db-client      |
            |                           +-----------+-----------+
            v                                       | issues
  +---------------------+   reads paths   +----------v----------+
  | <name>-db-connection|<----------------| Secret tls.crt/key  |
  | Secret (DSN+ssl_*)  |   (not bytes)   | ca.crt              |
  +----------+----------+                 +----------+----------+
             | OS_DATABASE__CONNECTION               | mounted RO
             v                                       v
  +---------------------------------------------------------------+
  | Keystone Deployment + 5 db_sync Jobs + Bootstrap Job          |
  |   /etc/keystone/db-tls/{ca.crt,tls.crt,tls.key}               |
  +-----------------------------+---------------------------------+
                                | TLS (mode-gated verify)
                                v
              +-----------------+------------------+
              | MaxScale (TLS)  -->  MariaDB Galera |  spec.tls.required=true
              | serverCASecretRef / clientCASecretRef <- DB CA Issuer
              +------------------------------------+
